speechOptions.instruction: Speak in a bright, warm, hopeful tone — like happily introducing something you love to a friend. Gentle, positive energy throughout; never dark or flat.

theme.bgGradient: radial-gradient(1200px 700px at 12% -10%, rgba(253,224,71,.30), transparent 60%), radial-gradient(1000px 600px at 100% 0%, rgba(251,146,60,.22), transparent 55%), linear-gradient(160deg, #FFFBF2, #FFF3DC 55%, #FCE8C8)

title: Collection Creation Demo — Weather Collection
description: Building a weather collection from scratch with MulmoClaude's Collections feature, using nothing but conversation.

beats.text: Every business trip, you end up checking the weather for each destination — city by city, hour by hour. With MulmoClaude, one conversation builds you this trip timeline.

html: <div class='w-2/5 flex flex-col justify-center px-10 space-y-6'>

Traveling for work,
still checking the weather
city by city?
<div class='rounded-xl p-5' style='background: rgba(255, 255, 255, .6); border: 1px solid rgba(234, 88, 12, .2)'>
The prompt we typed
"Show the weather as a timeline that follows my travel schedule."
→ One conversation builds this screen
1 / 16

beats.text: In this demo, we'll build it from scratch with MulmoClaude's Collections feature.

slide.title: A Weather Collection
slide.subtitle: Build your own weather tracker, {primary:just by talking}

beats.text: Getting started is easy. Open a terminal, type the one line on screen, and your browser opens with MulmoClaude ready to go.

html: <div class='relative flex items-center justify-center h-full' style='background: linear-gradient(160deg, #FFFBF2, #FFF3DC 55%, #FCE8C8)'>
How to launch

<div data-animation='animate' data-sync='1:0' data-opacity='0,1' data-scale='0.95,1' data-start='1.94' data-end='2.44' data-easing='easeOut' class='bg-gray-950 rounded-2xl p-10 border border-gray-700 max-w-2xl mx-auto shadow-2xl'>
npx mulmoclaude@latest
No complicated setup — this one line is all it takes.
3 / 16

beats.text: Let's get building. MulmoClaude's features line up in the toolbar at the top of the screen. Click the Collections button there, and the collections screen opens.

html: <div class='relative flex flex-col h-full' style='background: linear-gradient(160deg, #FFFBF2, #FFF3DC 55%, #FCE8C8)'>
STEP 1
Open Collections from the toolbar
Every feature lives in the toolbar at the top
<div class='flex-1 flex flex-col items-center justify-center px-6 pb-4'>
↑ Click this Collections button
⬆ Zoomed in on this strip
4 / 16

beats.text: Here's the collections screen. To create a new one, click the plus-Collection button in the top right.

html: <div class='relative flex flex-col h-full' style='background: linear-gradient(160deg, #FFFBF2, #FFF3DC 55%, #FCE8C8)'>
STEP 2
Top right of the collections screen
<div class='flex-1 flex items-center justify-center px-6 pb-4'>
5 / 16

beats.text: A screen appears asking how you'd like to create it. Pick Guided setup at the top — it asks you, question by question, what you want to keep track of.

html: <div class='relative flex flex-col h-full' style='background: linear-gradient(160deg, #FFFBF2, #FFF3DC 55%, #FCE8C8)'>
STEP 3
Answer a few questions and the collection builds itself
<div class='flex-1 flex items-center justify-center px-6 pb-4'>
6 / 16

beats.text: Choosing guided setup changes the screen to this. On the left, your conversation with the AI. Whatever comes out of it shows up immediately on the right — this time, a form asking what we want to track. Everything happens in this one screen.

html: <div class='relative flex flex-col h-full' style='background: linear-gradient(160deg, #FFFBF2, #FFF3DC 55%, #FCE8C8)'>
How to read the screen
Talk on the left, results on the right
The whole exchange happens in this one screen
<div class='flex-1 flex items-center justify-center px-6 pb-4'>
① Chat on the left
② Results appear right beside it
7 / 16

beats.text: We fill in what we want: the weather for the places we travel to for work — Tsuchiura, Tokyo, Morioka — all in one view, organized by time of day. That's all it takes.

html: <div class='relative flex flex-col h-full' style='background: linear-gradient(160deg, #FFFBF2, #FFF3DC 55%, #FCE8C8)'>
STEP 4
Write what you want in the form
"I travel for work, so I want the weather for several cities at a glance"
<div class='flex-1 flex items-center justify-center px-6 pb-4'>
8 / 16

beats.text: And with that, everything a weather collection needs was designed automatically — each city's conditions, highs and lows, and the chance of rain by time of day. The AI thought it all through. Now look at the bottom: it even suggests what to do next.

html: <div class='relative flex flex-col h-full' style='background: linear-gradient(160deg, #FFFBF2, #FFF3DC 55%, #FCE8C8)'>
Auto-generated
All the pieces, designed for you
From the data design to the next step, it's all suggested
<div class='flex-1 flex items-center justify-center px-6 pb-4'>
The AI's suggestion
9 / 16

beats.text: We just answer, "Yes, please!" The weather for all three cities is fetched automatically and lined up in a table.

html: <div class='relative flex flex-col h-full' style='background: linear-gradient(160deg, #FFFBF2, #FFF3DC 55%, #FCE8C8)'>
STEP 5
Ride along with the suggestion, and three cities line up
<div class='flex-1 flex items-center justify-center px-6 pb-4'>
<div class='absolute rounded-2xl px-6 py-3 shadow-xl whitespace-nowrap' style='top: 58%; left: 50%; transform: translateX(-50%); background: #FFF3DC; border: 1px solid rgba(234, 88, 12, .45)'>
10 / 16

beats.text: Click any city to see its details. Here's Morioka — a full week of forecasts, with the weather, temperatures, and rain chances neatly lined up.

html: <div class='relative flex flex-col h-full' style='background: linear-gradient(160deg, #FFFBF2, #FFF3DC 55%, #FCE8C8)'>
Details
Check each city in detail
A 7-day forecast is fetched automatically too
<div class='flex-1 flex items-center justify-center px-6 pb-4'>
11 / 16

beats.text: But when you're traveling, daily forecasts aren't enough — you want the weather hour by hour. Even after it's built, no problem. We asked, "Can you add 3-hourly forecasts?" — and it brought in another weather service to fetch the finer data.

html: <div class='relative flex items-center justify-center h-full' style='background: linear-gradient(160deg, #FFFBF2, #FFF3DC 55%, #FCE8C8)'>
We want finer-grained weather

<div data-animation='animate' data-sync='0:0.3' data-opacity='0,1' data-translate-x='-40,0' data-start='2.04' data-end='2.54' data-easing='easeOut' class='backdrop-blur rounded-2xl p-8' style='background: rgba(255, 255, 255, .6); border: 1px solid rgba(234, 88, 12, .2)'>
Japan's weather agency

<div data-animation='animate' data-sync='2:0.15' data-opacity='0,1' data-translate-x='40,0' data-start='8.72' data-end='9.22' data-easing='easeOut' class='backdrop-blur rounded-2xl p-8' style='background: rgba(255, 255, 255, .6); border: 1px solid rgba(234, 88, 12, .2)'>
+ a global weather service
Temperature, rain, and wind too
→ Even after it's built, you can combine the best of both!
12 / 16

beats.text: The 3-hourly forecast is in. All three cities now carry detailed data for today and tomorrow. A collection you've already built keeps growing like this — through conversation alone.

html: <div class='relative flex flex-col h-full' style='background: linear-gradient(160deg, #FFFBF2, #FFF3DC 55%, #FCE8C8)'>
Grow it later
Ask after the fact, and it's reflected right away
<div class='flex-1 flex items-center justify-center px-6 pb-4'>
13 / 16

beats.text: Finally we said, "Show the weather as a timeline that follows my travel schedule" — and there it is, the trip timeline from the start of this video. Leave Tsuchiura at seven, reach Tokyo by eight thirty. Leave Tokyo at three, arrive in Morioka at six. The weather for every leg, at a glance.

html: <div class='relative flex flex-col h-full' style='background: linear-gradient(160deg, #FFFBF2, #FFF3DC 55%, #FCE8C8)'>
STEP 6
Follow-up requests grew it this far
<div class='flex-1 flex items-center justify-center px-6 pb-4'>
<div class='absolute rounded-2xl px-6 py-4 shadow-xl' style='top: 38%; left: 71%; width: 27%; background: #FFF3DC; border: 1px solid rgba(234, 88, 12, .45)'>
💬 "Show the weather as a timeline that follows my travel schedule"
14 / 16

beats.text: Everything you just watched happened through conversation alone. Say what you want, check the result, ask for a little more. That loop is all it took to build a weather tracker — without writing a single line of code.

html: <div class='relative flex flex-col items-center justify-center h-full' style='background: linear-gradient(160deg, #FFFBF2, #FFF3DC 55%, #FCE8C8)'>
Built with conversation alone
<div data-animation='animate' data-sync='1:0' data-opacity='0,1' data-translate-x='-30,0' data-start='4.15' data-end='4.65' data-easing='easeOut' class='flex items-center gap-6'>
Step 1
<div data-animation='animate' data-sync='1:0.17' data-opacity='0,1' data-translate-x='-30,0' data-start='4.79' data-end='5.29' data-easing='easeOut' class='flex items-center gap-6'>
Step 2
Start a new one
<div data-animation='animate' data-sync='1:0.33' data-opacity='0,1' data-translate-x='-30,0' data-start='5.4' data-end='5.9' data-easing='easeOut' class='flex items-center gap-6'>
Step 3
Choose guided setup
<div data-animation='animate' data-sync='1:0.5' data-opacity='0,1' data-translate-x='-30,0' data-start='6.05' data-end='6.55' data-easing='easeOut' class='flex items-center gap-6'>
Step 4
Say what you want
"Weather for my trips, at a glance"
<div data-animation='animate' data-sync='1:0.67' data-opacity='0,1' data-translate-x='-30,0' data-start='6.7' data-end='7.2' data-easing='easeOut' class='flex items-center gap-6'>
Step 5
Fetch the weather
<div data-animation='animate' data-sync='1:0.83' data-opacity='0,1' data-translate-x='-30,0' data-start='7.31' data-end='7.81' data-easing='easeOut' class='flex items-center gap-6'>
Step 6
Grow it freely
No code. And it keeps growing — through conversation.
15 / 16

beats.text: Check the weather for the places you visit most, automatically, every day. Pull destinations from your calendar. Get a summary at six every morning. All of that is within reach. A collection isn't finished when it's built — it keeps growing as you talk with it. That's when the fun really starts. Give it a try!

html: <div class='relative flex flex-col items-center justify-center h-full' style='background: linear-gradient(160deg, #FFFBF2, #FFF3DC 55%, #FCE8C8)'>
Where it goes from here

<div data-animation='animate' data-sync='0:0.15' data-opacity='0,1' data-translate-y='30,0' data-start='0.72' data-end='1.22' data-easing='easeOut' class='backdrop-blur rounded-2xl p-7 flex items-start gap-5' style='background: rgba(255, 255, 255, .6); border: 1px solid rgba(234, 88, 12, .2)'>
Weather for your usual places
Home, office, and travel spots, checked daily

<div data-animation='animate' data-sync='1:0' data-opacity='0,1' data-translate-y='30,0' data-start='4.78' data-end='5.28' data-easing='easeOut' class='backdrop-blur rounded-2xl p-7 flex items-start gap-5' style='background: rgba(255, 255, 255, .6); border: 1px solid rgba(234, 88, 12, .2)'>
Weather fetched for wherever your plans are

<div data-animation='animate' data-sync='2:0' data-opacity='0,1' data-translate-y='30,0' data-start='7.04' data-end='7.54' data-easing='easeOut' class='backdrop-blur rounded-2xl p-7 flex items-start gap-5' style='background: rgba(255, 255, 255, .6); border: 1px solid rgba(234, 88, 12, .2)'>
Today's destinations and their weather, delivered

<div data-animation='animate' data-sync='4:0' data-opacity='0,1' data-translate-y='30,0' data-start='11.72' data-end='12.22' data-easing='easeOut' class='backdrop-blur rounded-2xl p-7 flex items-start gap-5' style='background: rgba(255, 255, 255, .6); border: 1px solid rgba(234, 88, 12, .2)'>
Still growable after it's built
Fields and views alike — one conversation away
Give it a try!
16 / 16
